title: • - [ ] **Event-driven architecture**:
content:
• - [ ] **Event-driven architecture**:
  • - Adopt event-driven patterns (e.g., publish-subscribe) for loosely coupled systems.
  • - Use a message broker like Kafka or RabbitMQ.

  • ---

  • ### **3. CI/CD Pipelines**
  • Automate workflows for building, testing, and deploying software:
  • - [ ] **Build pipelines**:
  • - Validate code quality with linters, formatters, and static analysis.
  • - Check for dependency vulnerabilities using tools like Dependabot or Snyk.
  • - [ ] **Test automation**:
  • - Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests.
  • - Use mocking or sandbox environments for external dependencies.
  • - [ ] **Deployment strategies**:
  • - Implement blue-green or canary deployments for safe rollouts.
  • - Automate rollback in case of failures.

  • ---

  • ### **4. Security and Compliance**
  • Proactively address vulnerabilities and compliance requirements:
  • - [ ] **Authentication and authorization**:
  • - Use OAuth 2.0, SSO, or OpenID Connect for consistent authentication.
  • - Enforce RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) and least privilege principles.
context: From document: full best practices house keeping; — chunk 21 (chunk 21/24)
